title: Māraṇa-karma
century: Medieval (c. 7th–14th c.); commentarial tradition to 18th c.
description: The sixth and most severe of the six ṣaṭ-karma — the transformation / death (*māraṇa*) rite, the most consequential of the *ugra*-operations, producing the targeted transformation or extinction of a hostile force. High caution — the final member of the "fierce four" (vidveṣaṇa, uccāṭana, māraṇa, plus the optional mohana). Distinct from the specific Agni-Māraṇa and Vīrudha-Āhāra-Māraṇa sub-sādhanas
